resources.string: Select a model in LLM Settings
Dedicated image upscaler with a gallery for upscaled results
Please select a model in settings
The document did not contain readable text.
Only PDF and text documents are supported.
Failed to attach media to message
FFmpeg is not available to prepare audio for llama chat
Failed to prepare audio for llama chat: %s
Describe the image you want to create…
Things to avoid in the image...
Describe the image you want to generate...
Number of CPU threads for generation
Lower = more like original, Higher = more creative
Another image generation task is already running.
Image generation failed with exit code %1$d.
The Stable Diffusion image binary is not available.
Select a model before starting image generation.
The selected model file is missing or unreadable.
Select a source image before starting img2img.
Select a source image before starting upscale.
The selected source image is missing or unreadable.
Note: GGUF VAEs are recommended for lower RAM usage.
FLUX models require text encoders (CLIP-L & T5) to understand prompts.
Required only if the model doesn\'t have a built-in VAE.
Flux models strictly require a VAE if not baked into the GGUF.
Warning: Flux might fail if VAE or Encoders are missing.
Select an upscaler model to see scaling options
Video file loaded. Audio extraction will happen on transcribe.
Whisper is not ready yet. Please try again in a moment.
Extract audio → Transcribe with Whisper → Summarize with LLM
No Whisper models - download one first
No LLM models - download one first
Combine multiple PDF files into one
Extract specific pages or split into parts
Extract all text content from PDF
Convert images into a PDF document
Reduce file size with 9 quality levels
Divide PDF into parts of max size
Combine multiple PDF files into one
Extract specific pages from a PDF
Enter page numbers or ranges separated by commas
Extract all text content from a PDF file
Text extracted and saved to Notes!
Extracted text will be saved to Notes
Extract text from images using ML Kit
Select multiple images to combine into a PDF
Reduce file size by compressing images
Divide PDF into parts with maximum file size
Choose where to store downloaded ZIM files. ZIM files can be large (100MB - 90GB), so select a folder with enough space.
Browse the catalog to download offline content
To share ZIM files over the network:
Download ZIMs from the Catalog tab
Open any URL in a browser to download ZIM files
Share ZIM files with other devices on your network. They can download files directly from this device.
ZIM files are stored in the app\'s internal storage for fast access by the Kiwix server.
ZIM files can be very large (100MB - 90GB). Make sure you have enough storage space.
Download, manage, and select chat models for AI conversations
Download Stable Diffusion models for image generation, video generation, and upscaling
Download speech recognition models for transcription
Share your models with other devices via WiFi
Are you sure you want to delete this model?
All models are stored locally on your device
Go to Discover to download or tap + to import
Models will appear here while downloading
This model supports vision capabilities (image input).
Would you like to download the vision projector file to enable this feature?
Scan QR or open URL to download models
View and control the Master\'s inference UI.
Save the current model settings and configuration as a preset.
Used for images, transcriptions, and upscaled videos
App will restart to apply language change
Unrestricted - AI tasks continue when screen is off
Restricted - AI tasks may pause when screen is off
Required for image and video generation to keep running when the screen turns off. Some devices may still pause work after a manual power-button lock.
App can keep image and video generation running when the screen turns off
⚠️ Some device manufacturers add extra battery restrictions. If unrestricted battery is already enabled and generation still pauses after locking, try the device-specific fix below.
Prevents automatic screen timeout while image or video generation is active. This helps with auto screen-off only and cannot override a manual power-button lock.
Enabled - Use models directly from SD card/Downloads
Disabled - Models will be copied to app storage
Required for using large models without copying them
✓ Models can be used directly from external storage
⚠️ Note: Native AI binaries can only access app-specific directories. Custom folders are not supported on Android 11+.
Export or restore app data, settings, native chats, Organizer data, generated media, and imported model files
Creates or restores the raw app databases, app settings, and generated media. Restoring replaces current app data and restarts the app.
Exports native Llama chats, servers, folders, prompt profiles, notes, calendar events, alarms, images, audio, and imported model files as a mergeable ZIP.
✓ Backup restored! The app will restart.
No database files were found to back up.
Cannot open the backup destination for writing.
Cannot open the selected backup file.
No valid database files were found in the backup.
The restored backup is missing the %1$s database file.
The integrity check for %1$s returned no result.
This will replace ALL current data (chats, notes, agents, pets) with the backup data. This cannot be undone.\n\nThe app will restart after restoring.
💡 Back up regularly before updating the app. Downloaded catalog model records and model files are skipped; imported local model records and generated media are kept.
⚠️ Quantized cache uses less memory but may reduce accuracy
⚡ ON: Faster inference, but slower startup and uses more RAM. Model must fit in memory.
💾 OFF (default): Model loads on-demand from storage. Slower but can run larger models.
Number of CPU threads for audio transcription
• More threads = faster transcription but more battery usage
• 4-6 threads is recommended for most devices
• Transcriptions are auto-saved to Notes Manager
Perform an action to see logs here
Run image or video generation to collect persistent diagnostics here.
Start a worker to see RPC server output
Active generation when the app ended: %1$s
No live app logs yet, but generation diagnostics are available below.
Create a note or transcribe audio to get started
Are you sure you want to delete \"%s\"?
Cannot open the note export destination.
Unknown notes or chat import format.
Plan events and optional phone alarms
Calendar and alarm tools also need to be enabled per chat before an LLM can use them.
End time must be after start time.
WebUI: ⬅️ = navigate within page | 🏠 = exit (keeps running) | ❌ = stop server
Installing all tools uses about 35 GB of storage and can take a while.
Install ONCE before Open WebUI, Oobabooga, FastSD CPU, and A1111
⚠️ Install this FIRST if you plan to use tools marked with 🐍
Install AI tools one by one or all at once over an Ubuntu SSH server
Visible from other devices on your network
Only this device can reach the server
%1$s still appears to be running after the stop command
Browse and manage files for installed tools
4. Install the SSH server inside Ubuntu:
6. Allow root login, password auth, and choose a port:
Use the Ubuntu SSH host, port, username, and password here. This guide now defaults to port 8025 for Ubuntu SSH.
Example one-liner password: termux. Change it after setup.
Start Ubuntu SSH from Termux in the background with the default port:
Connect to the Ubuntu SSH server. Host, port, username, and password stay manual, and the default port is 8025.
Select an Ollama model before starting native chat.
Are you sure you want to remove this message from the chat?
This will remove all later messages and resend this message with its saved attachments.
Error in transcribing, do you want to retry the transcription and send the message?
No chats in this folder yet
Could not create folder. Use a unique name.
Delete \"%1$s\"? Chats in this folder will move to Unfiled.
Delete \"%1$s\"? Affected chats (%2$s) will move to Unfiled.
No folders yet. Create one to organize chats.
Could not save profile. Use a unique name and non-empty prompt.
Delete \"%1$s\"? Existing chats keep their copied prompt.

You are a careful research assistant. When tools are enabled, use web_search for broad discovery, then use fetch_url on promising or authoritative URLs for deep reading; use Kiwix when offline sources are useful. Iterate through results, compare sources, separate facts from uncertainty, and cite URLs. For any meaningful research task, create a substantial note with sections, findings, open questions, and source citations; read and update that note as you learn so the research is not forgotten. Use todo lists for follow-up work, calendar events for deadlines, alarms for requested reminders, calculator for arithmetic, date/time for scheduling context, and image generation only when visual output helps.

You are an expert software engineer. Read existing context first, reason from the system in front of you, make minimal safe changes, explain important trade-offs, and include verification steps. When tools are enabled, use notes for investigation logs and decisions, todos for implementation checklists, web_search plus fetch_url for current API or bug research, Kiwix for offline references, calculator for estimates, and calendar/alarms for user-requested milestones.

You are a patient study tutor. Break topics into steps, check understanding, give examples, and adapt difficulty to the learner. When tools are enabled, create large study notes with summaries, examples, exercises, mistakes to review, and sources; use todo lists for practice plans, calendar events and alarms for study sessions, web_search/fetch_url/Kiwix for references, calculator for worked problems, and date/time for schedules.

You are a concise assistant. Answer directly, avoid filler, and include only the details needed to complete the user\'s task. When tools are enabled, use the smallest useful tool call: web_search then fetch_url for current facts, notes for durable results, todos for action lists, calendar/alarms for requested reminders, calculator for arithmetic, and date/time for scheduling.

You are a knowledge curator. Turn messy information into durable, well-structured notes. When tools are enabled, search broadly, fetch full sources, cite URLs, create big notes with headings and source lists, update existing notes instead of duplicating them, and use todos for unresolved follow-ups.

You are a personal organizer. Help the user turn plans into clear notes, todo lists, calendar events, and alarms. When tools are enabled, list/read existing notes, events, and alarms before editing; create or update Organizer items directly when the user asks; confirm important times and use device-local timezone unless the user gives another timezone.

You are a project planner. Convert goals into phases, risks, deliverables, todos, and milestones. When tools are enabled, create substantial planning notes, maintain todo lists, add calendar events for deadlines, set alarms only when reminders are requested, and use web_search plus fetch_url for external constraints or current references.

You are a meeting secretary. Capture decisions, action items, owners, dates, and follow-ups. When tools are enabled, create a detailed meeting note, update it as the discussion evolves, create todo lists for action items, add calendar events or alarms for agreed dates, and cite fetched sources when external references are used.

You are a creative image director. Improve vague visual ideas into strong prompts with subject, composition, style, lighting, palette, medium, and constraints. When image tools are enabled, generate, inspect if vision review is available, iterate when needed, and save useful prompts or selected outputs into notes only when it helps the user.

You are a data analyst. Clarify assumptions, calculate carefully, show concise reasoning, and separate observed data from inference. When tools are enabled, use calculator for arithmetic, date/time for time windows, web_search and fetch_url for current datasets or definitions, and create substantial notes with methods, results, caveats, and source citations.
Unknown chat or note import format.
Could not save chat as note: %1$s
Exported %1$d chats, %2$d notes, %3$d events, %4$d alarms, %5$d media files, and %6$d imported models
Imported %1$d chats, %2$d notes, %3$d events, %4$d alarms, %5$d media files, and %6$d imported models

This will add the chats, server settings, prompt profiles, folders, notes, calendar events, alarms, images, audio, and imported model files from the ZIP. Downloaded model records are not included in these backups. Existing app data will not be deleted.
Allow this chat to call enabled app tools.
Search the web and read result pages.
Search the configured offline Kiwix server.
Read public HTTP or HTTPS pages.
Read whitelisted note IDs, create notes, and edit notes directly.
Read whitelisted todo IDs, create lists, and update/check items directly.
Create, read, update, and delete Organizer calendar events when Organizer access is also allowed.
Create, read, update, and delete one-shot phone alarms when Organizer access is also allowed.
Generate PNG images with the native chat image preset.
If the server supports vision, let the LLM inspect generated images and regenerate better versions before answering.
New chats started after selecting this server inherit these tool switches. Existing chats keep their own settings.
Read the current local date and time.
Battery optimization may slow native chat while the phone is locked. Exempt the app for best locked-screen generation speed.
Context size will be automatically determined by the server.
Run scheduled prompts with tools and keep results in logs.
Choose a saved or default prompt
Shorter months clamp to their last valid day.
Completed in %1$s. Tap to view logs.
Stopped after %1$s. Tap to view logs.
Task name and prompt are required.
No native Llama server is configured.
No selected local model is available to start llama-server.
Local llama-server did not become ready within 3 minutes.
🎨 Generate images with Stable Diffusion & FLUX
📄 Process PDFs with OCR and AI
Allow unrestricted battery usage so image and video generation can keep running when the screen turns off automatically. Manual power-button locks may still depend on your device.
Choose where to save generated images, transcriptions, and videos
You can skip this and change it later in Settings
Required to use AI models directly from your SD card or Downloads folder without copying them.
• Use models directly from SD card
• No need to copy large files
You can skip this and models will be copied instead
Android 12+ limits background processes, which can kill AI tasks. Use ADB to disable this.
Go to Settings → Developer Options → Disable child process restrictions
ADB Commands (run on PC with phone connected):
Skip this if AI tasks complete without issues
Buy me a coffee on Ko-fi
This app uses the following open source projects:
SDAI team - Local diffusion Android reference and inspiration
Thank you for using Doomsday AI Toolbox!
Offline Wikipedia and ZIM file server
Local LLM runner with API compatibility
Experimental Stable Diffusion WebUI workflow based on AUTOMATIC1111
Default: Summarizes extracted PDF text concisely
Default: Combines chunk summaries into a cohesive document summary
⚠️ Select a model in LLM Settings first
ℹ️ Runs llama-bench with --progress. Results are saved and shown automatically when you return.
Delete all saved benchmark results for this model?
Choose the remote server used for PDF summarization
Larger context allows processing more text at once
Used for client-side chunking. Ollama also receives it per request; llama-server may keep its own runtime context.
More threads = faster processing, but higher CPU usage
Lower = more focused, Higher = more creative
Maximum length of the generated summary
Set a limit for each llama-cli call. Use 0 to disable the timeout.
Set a limit for each remote summary call. Use 0 to disable the timeout.
Timeout is disabled. Long summaries will keep running until they finish or you cancel them.
Choose whether PDF summary calls go to Ollama or llama-server.
Requested context %1$d tokens exceeds the last known server context of %2$d tokens.
Disabled by default for cleaner summaries. Enable only if your server/model needs it.
Free text target language or style, for example Spanish, English, or Portuguese (Brazil).
Choose an Ollama model before summarizing.
Set the llama-server URL before summarizing.

• Load/Save threads handle disk I/O
• Process threads handle the actual upscaling
• Lower values = less memory, slower processing
• Higher values = more memory, faster processing
Not connected to SSH. Go back and connect first.
Local only (enable remote access in Settings)
Connect multiple phones to run large models together
Enable to allow other devices on your network to access the Kiwix offline library
Open any URL in a browser to download ZIM files
Share ZIM files with other devices on your network. They can download files directly from this device.
Plan approved by user. Proceed with implementation.
User denied execution of %s. Please stop or try another way.
Optionally explain why you are denying this action. This helps the AI understand and adjust.
🤖 AI Agent ready for project **%1$s** (/workspace/%2$s/).\n\nConnect to the agent workspace to start coding.
Enter a name for your project.
Opening a persistent SSH shell in this project…
Terminal ready. Run a command to start working inside this workspace.
There are no active shell sessions in this project.
This will close all active SSH shell sessions for:
This only stops project shell sessions. It does not stop the main AI agent run.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s?
This helps the AI understand how to use the tool
You are the Debugger agent. Your job is to...
Helps Orchestrator understand when to use this agent
Load a pre-made template to learn how to create a custom tool.
Load a pre-made template to learn how to create a custom agent.
Enter a description for this snapshot:
SSH to isolated ai-agent proot. Uses Ollama API for LLM inference with tool calling.
Auto-approve safe file writes and plans.
⚠️ Auto-approve run_command calls. Use with extreme caution — may execute dangerous commands!
Read file content in line-numbered chunks
The agent has access to these tools to help you:
Configure model and system prompt for each agent
Choose API. Ollama has local management tools; llama-server is lighter.
Note: Model, Threads and KV settings are managed by llama-server via CLI arguments, not here.
This role keeps its own override for Ollama. Active llama-server model: %1$s.
Before you start chatting with the AI:
Alerts when the AI agent needs your input or approval.
The agent is waiting on a user decision to continue.
The agent is waiting for the proposed plan to be approved or adjusted.
The agent is waiting for a response before it can continue.
Auto-compacts at %1$d%% of the context window.
Older history compacted automatically (%1$d turns omitted).
Raw estimate %1$d tokens. Auto-compacts at %2$d%% of the window.
Normalized prompt estimate %1$d tokens. History compaction starts at %2$d%% of the window.
Using the hard-compacted retained basis. Current prompt %1$d → %2$d tokens, with re-compaction gated until new turn growth or %3$d%% emergency pressure.
Actual backend usage: prompt %1$d, completion %2$s, total %3$s tokens.
The previous response attempted a tool call, but the call was malformed.
First emit {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": {\"path\": \"brain/tools_reference.md\"}}, then retry with a proper structured tool call outside <think>.
The previous response produced no visible answer and no structured tool call.
First emit {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": {\"path\": \"brain/tools_reference.md\"}}, then reply with either a concise answer or a proper structured tool call outside <think>.
Project memory must be updated before this task can finish.
Call write_memory or the Summarizer to record what changed and why, then retry finish_task.
Reflection found missing work or unresolved risks, so finalization is blocked for now.
Address the missing items, update memory if needed, verify the changed files or commands, and only then try to finish again.

Before finishing, use the reflection result to address the missing plan items and risks. Read plan.md, changed_files.md, and any relevant files or command outputs, do the missing work, then retry reflection or finish_task.
Are you sure you want to delete project \"%s\"? All workspace files will be removed.
2️⃣ Create a new project by tapping the 📁 icon in the top-left corner
3️⃣ Select a model and start chatting!
The project tells the AI where your files are.
Reloading the selected project conversation and workspace…
Select or create a project conversation to start working with the AI agent.
Select or create a project conversation before browsing the workspace.
The AI agent needs the SSH server connected before chat and workspace actions can work.
This will permanently delete the conversation history.
🚨 ALSO REMOVING FILES IN PROOT ENVIRONMENT:
FontSize: Text size in pixels (16-48 recommended)
Upscaled file not found in cache
Could not save to output folder. Video saved in cache.
Save error: %s. Video may be in cache.
No output folder set. Video saved to cache: %s
Go to Discover or tap + to import
Model linked from external storage (no copy needed)
Tip: Grant \'All files access\' in Settings to use models without copying
Use the dedicated ONNX models card to import SDAI-style ORT bundles.
Manage SDAI-style ORT bundles for local txt2img
SDAI-style ORT bundles for low-RAM local diffusion
All files access improves local model linking
Granting all files access lets the app link compatible ONNX bundles in place instead of copying them into the managed ADT folder.
No ONNX image bundles installed yet.
Switch between the original SDAI catalog and the ManuXD32 fork that adds VAE encoders for img2img-capable bundles.
No ONNX bundle downloads are running.
Could not open the selected folder.
Imported %1$s into the managed ADT folder.
This will remove %1$s from the device.
SDAI-style txt2img and img2img with local ORT bundles
This screen works with SDAI-style ORT bundles that include text encoder, UNet, VAE decoder, and tokenizer files. img2img-capable bundles also need vae_encoder/model.ort.
Grant all files access if you want the app to reuse bundles directly from shared storage, or keep using copied bundles from the managed ADT folder.
Choose the installed ORT bundle for local txt2img.
Choose an installed ORT bundle. Classic ONNX img2img requires a bundle that also includes vae_encoder/model.ort.
Switch between fresh text generation and classic latent img2img editing.
Positive and negative prompts are encoded locally through the bundle text encoder.
Pick a source image, then tune how strongly generation should move away from it. The source is fit into a padded 512x512 canvas so it keeps its aspect ratio instead of being stretched.
This bundle is txt2img-only. ONNX img2img in this app needs a bundle that also includes vae_encoder/model.ort.

ONNX img2img always fits your source image into a white 512x512 square before VAE encoding. Non-square inputs get padding instead of being stretched, which preserves composition and avoids deformation on mobile.
img2img always runs on a fixed padded 512x512 source canvas. Width and height are locked in this mode so the input keeps its aspect ratio without deformation.
Use direct numeric inputs. The app accepts any size from 64 upward and normalizes it to the nearest multiple of 8 before generation.
High-value ONNX settings that are usually safe to tweak without diving into provider internals. These defaults are the closest path to SDAI parity.
Lower-level ONNX Runtime and NNAPI controls for fine tuning sessions on compatible devices. Changing them can improve compatibility, but it can also move results away from SDAI parity.

img2img uses the requested steps as the full schedule, then strength decides how much of that schedule stays active. Lower strength preserves more of the source image and therefore uses fewer active denoise steps.
Use around 0.20 to 0.45 for recolors and targeted edits, 0.50 to 0.70 for stronger restyling, and 0.75 or more only when you want the result to drift toward a near redraw.
NNAPI is only used for UNet and VAE. If session creation fails, the app retries on CPU and shows a warning after generation.

SDAI-aligned defaults: keep CPU as the main backend, leave per-component overrides on Default, keep sequential execution, and only change thread settings if you are debugging performance or device compatibility.
Expert overrides can help on some phones, but they may also change execution order, provider behavior, or memory handling enough to reduce parity with SDAI.
ONNX diffusion works on 8-pixel latent grids, so this is the exact size that will be used during generation.
Requested %1$d x %2$d. The runtime will snap it to the nearest 8-pixel grid size shown above.
Start with 512x512, around 20 steps, and CPU on smaller devices. Use NNAPI only if the device handles the model reliably.
No ONNX images match this filter yet.
An ONNX generation is already running.
Enter a prompt before starting generation.
CFG scale must be greater than 0 and at most 30.
Select a source image before starting ONNX img2img.
The selected ONNX bundle does not support img2img.
Failed to prepare the source image: %1$s
The image finished, but the shared output folder was not available for mirroring.
The image finished, but copying it to the shared output folder failed: %1$s
This will remove %1$s and its ONNX metadata.
Local ORT bundles in AI Doomsday Toolbox

Use extracted SDAI-style bundles with text_encoder, tokenizer, unet, and vae_decoder directories. ONNX img2img also needs vae_encoder/model.ort. The app loads the .ort files locally through ONNX Runtime.

Prompt is the main instruction the model should follow, so it affects subject choice, style, and prompt adherence the most. Negative prompt tells the model what to avoid, which usually improves adherence and reduces unwanted traits. Seed controls reproducibility and determinism: the same model, prompt, settings, and seed should stay much closer to the same composition than a random seed.

Width and height set the requested output size and strongly affect RAM use, speed, and composition. ONNX diffusion still runs on an 8-pixel latent grid, so the app shows the normalized runtime size that will actually be used. Larger normalized sizes raise RAM pressure and generation time, while smaller sizes are safer on low-RAM phones.

Steps mostly affect refinement time and detail. More steps can improve adherence and finish quality, but they also cost time. In img2img, the requested step count is still the full schedule, but strength decides how much of that schedule stays active. CFG scale changes how hard the model follows the prompt: too low can drift away, too high can cause harsh or unstable results.

Classic ONNX img2img starts from a source image, fits it into a white padded 512x512 canvas, encodes it into latents with the VAE encoder, then denoises toward your prompt. Lower strength preserves more of the source image, composition, and local colors. Higher strength redraws more of the scene, but it should still behave like a stronger edit instead of washing out the whole image.

Backend profile chooses the main execution provider. CPU is the safest and most predictable option for compatibility and prompt-parity checks. NNAPI can improve speed on some devices, but it may also change stability or fall back depending on drivers and model support. Runtime thread count affects CPU parallelism, so higher values can help speed but also raise heat and contention.

Graph optimization level changes how aggressively ONNX Runtime rewrites the graph before execution. This mostly affects speed and memory use, and sometimes stability on unusual bundles. Intra-op threads tune parallel work inside one operator, while inter-op threads tune work across operators. Execution mode changes whether ONNX Runtime stays sequential or tries parallel scheduling, which can alter performance and RAM pressure depending on the phone.

Per-component backend overrides let you force UNet, VAE decoder, or VAE encoder onto CPU or NNAPI independently, which mainly affects speed, memory pressure, and compatibility. Memory pattern optimization can reduce allocator churn on repeatable shapes. CPU arena allocator can improve throughput, but on some devices turning it off helps stability or lowers spikes.

Disable NNAPI CPU fallback tells ONNX Runtime not to quietly drop unsupported work back to CPU, which can improve debugging but increases failure risk. Prefer NNAPI FP16 asks the provider to prefer half precision when supported, which can improve speed and memory use but may slightly change numerical behavior. These options affect compatibility and crash risk more than artistic quality.
Start with 512x512 and around 20 steps on lower-RAM devices. CPU is the safest default. NNAPI can help on some phones, but incompatible models or drivers may still fall back to CPU.

Catalog installs are managed under Download/ADT/onnx. Imported bundles can stay linked in place or be copied there when direct filesystem access is unavailable. Finished ONNX images keep their app-local copy and are also mirrored into the shared output folder under images/onnx when you configure one, together with the JSON metadata sidecar.

The app expects extracted SDAI-style ORT bundles. Txt2img needs text_encoder, tokenizer, unet, and vae_decoder. Img2img also needs vae_encoder/model.ort. If any of those parts are missing, the feature that depends on them is disabled because the pipeline would no longer match the expected local diffusion contract.

Prompt text is tokenized locally, the text encoder builds CLIP embeddings, the UNet denoises latents step by step, and the VAE decodes the final latent back into an image. This means speed, RAM use, and prompt adherence depend on the whole pipeline, not only on the checkpoint itself.

Prompt is the strongest quality and adherence control in the whole UI. Add the subject, style, medium, composition, and any must-have traits directly here. Longer prompts can help when they stay coherent, but once the important parts exceed the 77-token CLIP window the tail is truncated, so put the most important instructions first.

Negative prompt tells the model what to suppress. It is most useful for removing unwanted rendering styles, extra anatomy, realism, or artifacts. A well-targeted negative prompt usually improves prompt adherence, but an over-aggressive one can also fight the positive prompt and flatten the image.

Width changes framing, composition space, speed, and RAM use. Wider images give the model more horizontal room but usually cost more memory. Raising width does not make the model smarter; it mostly changes canvas shape and compute cost.

Height affects the same things as width, but vertically. Tall canvases are useful for portraits or posters, while shorter canvases are easier on RAM. Bigger dimensions usually mean slower generations and more heat.

Stable Diffusion latents work on an 8-pixel grid, so the app snaps your requested size to the nearest valid runtime size. This changes speed, RAM, and the actual latent shape used by the scheduler. If prompt parity matters, always compare results using the normalized size, not only the requested size.

Steps mainly control how long the denoiser refines the image. More steps can improve structure, detail, and prompt adherence, but only up to a point. Very high values mostly cost time and battery. For SDAI-like behavior, start around 20 and only raise it when the result is still underdeveloped.

In img2img, the requested steps are the full schedule, but denoise strength determines how many of those steps remain active. This is why the app shows effective denoise steps separately. For small edits, low effective-step counts are usually a good sign because they preserve more of the original image instead of redrawing it.

CFG scale controls how strongly the conditional prompt pulls away from the unconditional prediction. Higher CFG usually increases prompt adherence, but too much can create brittle, overforced, or strange results. Lower CFG is often more natural, but it can drift away from the prompt.

Seed controls reproducibility. Reusing the same seed with the same model, prompts, and normalized size should keep the composition close enough to compare pipeline changes. Changing the seed is useful when the prompt is good but a specific sample is weak.

The source image gives img2img its initial structure. This app now fits that image inside a white padded 512x512 square before encoding, so wide and tall images keep their proportions instead of being stretched. Composition, silhouette, and broad color blocks survive best when the image already resembles the intended target.

The img2img canvas is fixed to 512x512 for stable local mobile behavior. Non-square images are scaled to fit inside that square and the remaining space is filled with opaque white padding. This preserves the original composition much better than forcing the image to stretch into a square.

Strength decides how early img2img starts in the denoising schedule. Lower strength keeps more of the source composition, details, and local colors. Higher strength gives the prompt more control and activates more of the denoising schedule, but it should act like a stronger redraw instead of burning the whole image. If the edit is simple, like changing one cloud to green, start low before adding more strength.

For targeted edits, keep the source image close to the intended result and start with low strength. Raising strength is useful when the source image is too far from the target, but it also makes the model spend more denoising budget on global changes instead of the one edit you actually asked for. Padding helps preserve framing, while lower strength helps preserve local structure.

The main backend profile mostly affects speed, driver compatibility, and numerical behavior. CPU is the safest reference path for SDAI-parity checks. NNAPI may be faster on some devices, but it can also introduce fallback behavior, provider quirks, or outright failures depending on the phone.

Runtime thread count mainly affects CPU speed and heat. Higher values can help on larger phones or tablets, but too many threads can increase contention, power use, and thermals. It rarely changes artistic quality directly, but it can change stability on weaker devices.

Graph optimization level tells ONNX Runtime how aggressively to rewrite the model graph before execution. Higher optimization usually improves speed and memory use, but if you are debugging parity or compatibility you may want to reduce it temporarily. It can indirectly change behavior when a provider handles optimized graphs differently.

Intra-op threads control parallel work inside a single operator. They mostly affect CPU throughput and heat. Raising them can help heavy operators, but on small devices they may increase thermal throttling faster than they help speed.

Inter-op threads control how many operators ONNX Runtime tries to run in parallel. This is mostly a performance and RAM tuning knob. Higher values can increase scheduling pressure and memory overhead, so they are usually an expert-only compatibility setting.

Execution mode chooses whether operators run sequentially or with more parallel scheduling. Sequential is the safest parity-friendly default because it keeps runtime behavior closer to the reference path. Parallel mode may improve performance on some devices, but it can also increase RAM pressure and make debugging harder.

UNet backend override is one of the most parity-sensitive expert controls because UNet does most of the denoising work. Changing it mainly affects speed and compatibility, but it can also change numerical behavior enough to alter prompt adherence or the final look.

VAE decoder override affects the final image decode stage. It has less effect on prompt understanding than UNet, but it can still change output crispness, compatibility, or stability depending on the provider.

VAE encoder override only matters for img2img. It affects how the source image is projected into latents before denoising starts. If you are debugging img2img parity, leave it on Default first so the encode stage stays close to the SDAI-aligned path.

Memory pattern optimization tries to reuse predictable allocation patterns. It usually affects speed and RAM churn more than image quality. Turning it off can help debug device-specific issues, but it may slightly reduce performance.

CPU arena allocator keeps memory pools around for reuse. That often improves throughput, but it can also create bigger peaks on some devices. If the phone is unstable, toggling this is a compatibility experiment, not a quality control.

Disabling NNAPI CPU fallback tells ONNX Runtime to fail instead of quietly routing unsupported work back to CPU. This is useful for debugging provider support, but it increases crash and failure risk. It does not improve artistic quality by itself.

Prefer NNAPI FP16 asks the provider to favor half precision when supported. This can reduce memory use and improve speed, but it may also slightly change numerical behavior and therefore move the result away from strict SDAI-like output.

For the closest SDAI-like baseline, use CPU as the main backend, leave component overrides on Default, keep sequential execution, start around 512x512 and 20 steps, and only change one control at a time while keeping the same seed.
How to stay closest to SDAI

If you are comparing this app to SDAI, match the exact model bundle, prompt, negative prompt, normalized size, steps, CFG, seed, and keep expert runtime tweaks at their defaults. When results differ, compare logs for prompt preprocessing, token counts, backend selection, and timestep scheduling before blaming the model itself.

Every finished ONNX generation keeps an app-local PNG and JSON sidecar so the gallery stays fast and self-contained. When a shared output folder is configured, the same PNG and metadata are mirrored into images/onnx/\u003cmode\u003e so exported files still carry their generation settings with them.
Transcribe audio/video, then summarize with LLM
Create fine-tuning datasets from PDFs and text files. Pipeline: Clean → Questions (5×) → Answers → Rating
Tap + to create your first dataset
\"%s\" and all its data will be permanently deleted.
Add PDFs or text files to start
Extracting PDF text; OCR fallback can take a while on scanned pages.
Splitting extracted text into dataset chunks...
Saving source and chunks to the dataset...
Import stopped before it could finish.
This import now runs in the Dataset foreground service, so you can leave this screen while it continues.
PDF pages: %1$d total, %2$d text, %3$d OCR, %4$d empty
Dataset project is still loading. Try again in a moment.
Could not open the selected file.
Build a dataset by importing text, cleaning it, generating questions, generating answers, reviewing quality, and exporting the approved Q&A pairs.
Add PDF or TXT files from the Sources tab. Imports run in the Dataset foreground service, so extraction, OCR fallback, chunking, and saving can continue after you leave this screen.
The normal order is Clean, Questions, Answers, then Rating. Use Run sequence to queue several stages at once, or run a single stage when you only need one step.
Use the Review tab to filter, edit, regenerate, delete, and export Q&A pairs. Export is inside the Actions menu; selected items are exported first, otherwise accepted scored answers are exported.
The active job appears in the progress banner and notification. The Queue tab only lists jobs waiting behind the active job, where you can reorder or remove them.

Choose the backend/model before processing. Final language can force generated questions, answers, and ratings into one language, or stay blank to follow the source. Prompt edits affect future jobs, while already saved answers and ratings stay unchanged until regenerated.
The selected chunks will be deleted, and any Q&A linked to those chunks will also be removed.
Selected stages are queued in the order shown. Stages with no available work will finish without changing data.
Waiting jobs appear below while this one runs.
The active job is shown in the progress banner; this list only shows jobs waiting behind it.
Choose Ollama or the llama.cpp server started from Dashboard
This backend uses the llama.cpp server managed from the Dashboard.
Leave blank to use the language from the source. Set a language to force questions, answers, and ratings into that language.
Foreground progress for Dataset Creator jobs
Select an Ollama model before running dataset jobs.
• FontSize: Text size in pixels (16-48 recommended)
Not connected to SSH. Please connect first.
Generated images will appear here.\nStart FastSD and generate some!
Are you sure you want to delete the selected images? This cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete %s?
Install tools from the Termux screen first
💡 Long-press to select files for download, or upload files from your phone into the current folder
Enter file URL (wget will download to current folder)
Make sure the server is running in Termux
Ollama - Run 7B, 13B models locally with an OpenAI-compatible API.
Open WebUI - Modern ChatGPT-like frontend for Ollama with RAG support.
Big-AGI - Professional AI frontend with Beam multi-model comparison.
Oobabooga - Powerful text generation web UI with multiple backends.
FastSD CPU - Ultra-fast image generation (SD Turbo) optimized for CPUs.
A1111 SD WebUI - The industry-standard web UI for Stable Diffusion.

Installs with shared Python 3.11, mirrored Stable Diffusion dependencies, and preseeded CLIP/OpenCLIP requirements. The Web UI and API run on port 7865 without auto-downloading the default SD 1.5 checkpoint or repeating environment setup on first launch.
ComfyUI - Node-based GUI for SD (best for low RAM and complex workflows).
Manu-Bot - Autonomous coding agent with multi-tool usage capability.
MCP Server - Model Context Protocol server for tool and data integration.
Kokoro TTS - High-quality offline Text-to-Speech engine.
Piper TTS - Efficient, natural sounding local neural TTS.
Leave blank for auto (based on RAM)
Master keeps 0 layers. All model layers will be sent to workers. Requires enough worker RAM for the entire model.
Speeds up repeated tokens in context
Significantly reduces memory usage and speeds up inference
Build the launch command from the current UI settings. Leave it blank to use the standard generated command.
Uses a small draft model to predict tokens faster
⚠️ Draft model (%dMB) loads on master device alongside main model
The following command will be executed:
Customize the command for the current run:
Start Worker mode on another device,\nthen tap \'Add Worker\' to enter their IP:PORT
Started with %d worker(s). Check dashboard for status.
The llama-server process stopped unexpectedly with exit code %1$d.
Add and enable at least one worker
Import or download a GGUF model first.
Enter the worker details from the Worker device\'s screen:
Look at the Worker device\'s screen for the IP and PORT values
Leave empty to allow all IPs
⚠️ Warning: All IPs can connect if they have the password.
Allow other devices to control the master
Connect to another master device to control it
Switch to %s on the remote master?
Connection failed. Check IP and password.
Cannot delete: model is currently active
Are you sure you want to stop the remote master server? It will terminate all active generation!
No LLM models - download one first
Extracted %1$d characters across %2$d pages (%3$d text-layer, %4$d OCR, %5$d empty).
The run failed after one retry. These completed chunk summaries are kept for inspection.
The remote summary run failed after one retry. These completed chunk summaries are kept for inspection.
No extractable text was found in this PDF.
The model returned an empty summary.
The model process exited with code %1$d.
The model process exited with code %1$d: %2$s
The model call timed out after %1$d minutes.
Combine batch %1$d of %2$d failed after retry: %3$s
Cache layers locally for faster reconnection
Enter this address in Master\'s \'Add Worker\' dialog
Larger models = better accuracy, slower speed
Scan QR or open URL to download models
Share your AI models\nwith other devices

1. Both devices must be on the same WiFi
2. Start the server above
3. On the other device, scan QR or open URL
4. Click on a model to download
Connect multiple phones to run large AI models together!\n\nEach device contributes RAM to load different layers of the model.
Host the model file and coordinate inference across connected workers.
Help process model layers for a master device. Choose how much RAM to share.
All devices must be on the same WiFi network. Performance depends on network speed.
RPC is unencrypted. Only use on trusted local networks.
Tip: Grant \'All files access\' in Settings to use models without copying
Model linked from external storage (no copy needed)
Could not open the selected model file.
Could not create the export file.
Are you sure you want to delete %s? This action CANNOT be undone.
REALLY delete %s? All stats, money, and items will be lost forever.
Unknown place, it could be dangerous, are you sure you wanna go there?
💤 Rest at home to recover energy
Choose normal study or a Pomodoro plan. Labels are optional and help Tama understand what you are working on.
Study until you manually stop, like before.
Focus and rest timers that keep running while the app is closed.
No saved labels yet. You can start without labels or create one below.
No finished study sessions in this range yet.
Total %1$s · Focus %2$s · Rest %3$s · +%4$d edu · %5$d completed · %6$d stopped
Buy healing potions to restore Tama’s health.
%1$s is too tired to travel. Needs %2$d energy.
The cozy main room where your pet lives.
A playful room with a TV, sofa, and game corner.
A bright room with plants, soft light, and a calm mood.
A quiet room with books, a reading nook, and soft light.
A warm kitchen with mint cabinets, hanging pans, and a bright window.
A dreamy moonlit room with a telescope, crystals, and hanging stars.
A cheerful studio with an easel, paints, and sunny creative clutter.
A clean modern room with a city window, sofa, plants, and a TV corner.
Pick a game and earn coins.
Each round is short, retro, and coin-paying.
A noisy old-school arcade full of coin games.
Step inside to choose a game and win coins.
Move left and right to collect falling retro items.
Use only left and right to collect the falling items.
Tap the arrows or use lane changes to move one step at a time.
This arcade hub is ready for more minigames later.
Only lane moves count. Catch the item before it hits the bottom.
Coins were added to your pet wallet.
Flip cards on a 4x4 board and find all eight pairs before 12 turns run out.
You cleared the board before the turn limit.
No toys yet. Buy some in the shop.
Tap place to choose the left or right slot in the main room.
Choose where this toy should stay in the main room.
This is the room your pet is using right now.
💡 Visit a Shop to buy more food!
Your cozy home. Rest and recover here.
Play retro games to win coins.
Buy transformation potions and strange brews.
There is no park encounter right now.
That park encounter is no longer available.
%1$s returned to the middle of the park.
You both started cleaning the park together.
%1$s and the recycler cleaned the whole park together. The place feels bright again.
%1$s helped clean the park and earned 200 coins from the recycler.
%1$s declined to help the recycler today.
Choose which crops to sell. This seller pays 25% more than the usual farm shop.
You do not have any crops to sell right now.
Only crops and produce can be sold here.
%1$s decided not to sell anything at the market.
Sold %1$d %2$s at the park market for %3$d coins.
That room could not be found.
Your pet is already using that room.
Your pet does not own that room yet.
Create local txt2vid and img2vid clips
Choose the model type for %1$s before downloading it.
No video generation models installed yet.
Describe the motion, scene, and style for the video...
Optional details to avoid in the video...
Failed to copy video generation info: %1$s
Raw key=value pairs passed directly to stable-diffusion.cpp for the selected cache mode.
Comma-separated 1 and 0 values. 1 forces compute, 0 allows caching on that step.
Caching is only available for diffusion sampling. Image upscale uses the dedicated upscale mode instead.
Recommended for checkpoint-style UNET models: start with ucache or spectrum.
Recommended for FLUX and other DiT image models: easycache, dbcache, taylorseer, cache-dit, or spectrum.
Recommended for current video diffusion models: easycache, dbcache, taylorseer, cache-dit, or spectrum.
This mode is mainly documented for DiT models. It may still work, but ucache or spectrum are usually the safer starting points here.
ucache is mainly documented for UNET models. FLUX-style DiT models usually pair better with the DiT-oriented modes or spectrum.
ucache is mainly documented for UNET models. Video DiT models usually pair better with easycache, dbcache, taylorseer, cache-dit, or spectrum.
Copying generated video to the output folder…

Battery optimization is still enabled. On some devices, image and video generation may pause when the screen turns off, and aggressive OEM restrictions can still affect manual locks. You can allow unrestricted battery now, open a device-specific fix guide, or continue anyway.
The previous app session ended during active generation. Check Debug Logs for the recorded exit reason.
Another video generation task is already running.
The Stable Diffusion video binary is not available.
Video generation failed with exit code %1$d.
MP4 conversion failed with exit code %1$d.
The generated AVI file was not found.
No output folder is configured, so the video stays in app storage only.
The output folder is unavailable, so the video stays in app storage only.
The video was generated, but exporting it failed: %1$s
Could not load the selected image: %1$s
The video generation service is not ready yet.
Select a video generation model first.
Select an input image for img2vid.
Choose a VAE model or disable the VAE toggle.
Choose a T5-XXL model or disable the T5-XXL toggle.
txt2img creates a new image from text alone. It is the cleanest mode when you want the model to invent the whole composition.
img2img starts from an input image, then redraws it toward your prompt. Lower strength keeps more of the source image, while higher strength changes it more aggressively.
Upscale uses the dedicated ESRGAN-style upscaler path, not diffusion sampling. It improves resolution and detail on an existing image.
Choose the main diffusion model or the ESRGAN-style upscaler depending on the mode. The selected model decides which companion models, edit flow, and runtime flags the run can use.
VAE controls latent decoding and final image reconstruction. Some FLUX setups effectively need it, while checkpoint models may use the built-in decoder when you leave it empty.
FLUX encoder pickers provide the extra text encoders FLUX-style models use. Missing components usually hurt prompt understanding or stop generation altogether.
Quantization changes the tensor precision used by stable-diffusion.cpp. Lower precision can save memory and sometimes speed things up, but it may reduce quality.

These are the classic checkpoint-style models. They are usually the easiest place to start, work well with img2img strength, and pair naturally with optional VAE, ControlNet, LoRA, and SDXL PhotoMaker.
SD3-family checkpoints use a DiT-style stack and require CLIP-L, CLIP-G, and T5-XXL side encoders. Use them when you want SD3 prompting behavior and are ready to install the full encoder set.
FLUX.1 uses `--diffusion-model` with VAE, CLIP-L, and T5-XXL. Upstream examples usually keep CFG around 1. Quantized FLUX variants are often the practical way to run it on lower-memory phones.

Kontext is the FLUX reference-edit family. It uses a reference image path instead of classic img2img strength, so it is best for guided edits where the source image should steer the result more directly.

FLUX.2 switches from T5/CLIP to an image-side LLM companion. It still uses reference-image editing for edit-capable variants, and smaller or quantized variants are the safer choice on constrained RAM.
Chroma families are DiT image models that use T5-XXL, with Chroma also requiring a VAE. The Chroma-specific mask toggle is only worth touching if upstream guidance or model notes call for it.
Qwen Image uses an LLM companion and can optionally use VAE or TAE. Upstream examples commonly enable `diffusion-fa` and use a flow shift around 3 when the model variant supports it.
Qwen Image Edit is another reference-image family. Variant 2509 also needs a vision projector, while variant 2511 requires the zero-cond-t flag for the upstream-recommended edit path.

Z-Image pairs a diffusion model with VAE and an LLM companion. Upstream specifically calls it out as workable in very low-memory environments, which makes it one of the best first picks for devices under 8 GB RAM.
Ovis-Image also uses the VAE + image-LLM pattern. Treat it like a DiT family that benefits from the same memory-oriented runtime flags as the other newer image models.
Anima uses VAE plus a compact Qwen-family image LLM. It is useful when you want a newer LLM-conditioned family without jumping to the largest companion-model stacks.

TAE and TAESD are lightweight decoder companions, not main image models. They can reduce decode overhead on supported families and are especially helpful when full VAE decoding is the part pushing memory too hard.
PhotoMaker is an SDXL-side adapter. Use it only with SDXL-compatible checkpoint stacks, and think of it as a specialized add-on rather than a replacement for the main diffusion model.
ESRGAN models are only for image upscale mode. They do not run diffusion sampling, so they do not use diffusion caching or the txt2img/img2img-only family controls.
The prompt describes the subject, framing, style, lighting, and details you want. More concrete prompts usually give more predictable results.
Negative prompt lists traits to avoid. It is useful for steering away from blur, extra limbs, bad anatomy, or unwanted styles.
Width and height set the output resolution. Larger sizes can improve composition flexibility but cost more time and memory.
Steps control how long the sampler refines the image. CFG scale controls how strongly the prompt is enforced. Higher values can improve prompt adherence but may also create artifacts.
Sampling method changes the denoising algorithm. Different samplers trade speed, detail, and stability differently, so the best choice depends on the model and look you want.
Seed controls reproducibility. Reusing the same seed with the same settings helps reproduce a previous result, while a random seed explores new variations.
Threads control CPU parallelism for the current run. More threads can be faster, but they can also increase heat, battery use, and contention with other work.
Caching reuses intermediate diffusion work between steps to speed up sampling. For checkpoint-style UNET models, ucache and spectrum are the usual starting points.
Caching can also accelerate FLUX and other DiT models. easycache, dbcache, taylorseer, cache-dit, and spectrum are the modes that best match this model family.
Cache option is passed through exactly as typed, so you can use any supported key=value pair combination from stable-diffusion.cpp without waiting for app-side schema updates.
SCM policy and SCM mask are cache-dit-only controls. They decide which steps are allowed to cache and whether those steps always cache or still check thresholds first.
Upscale does not use diffusion-step caching because the app runs stable-diffusion.cpp in dedicated upscale mode here, not txt2img or img2img sampling.

VAE tiling reduces decode-memory spikes by splitting VAE work into tiles. Enable it when decoding is the part that crashes or stalls, and tune tile size or overlap more conservatively on tight-memory phones.

Flow shift is a family-specific sampler control mostly used by newer DiT image families such as Qwen Image. Start with the upstream example value when documented, and only move it gradually because large changes can alter the model’s intended look.
diffusion-fa is a performance and memory flag, not a creative control. It can reduce memory use on supported builds, so it is worth trying first when a family is close to fitting in RAM.
mmap loads model data through memory mapping instead of trying to keep everything in ordinary RAM up front. On Android phones this is usually one of the safest first toggles for large models.
vae-conv-direct is another memory-oriented flag. Upstream recommends it when VAE or TAE decode still pushes memory too hard after you have already tried tiling or lighter decoder choices.
The input image gives img2img a visual starting point. The app pads non-square images to reduce unwanted cropping before they go into the model.
Strength controls how much img2img is allowed to move away from the source image. Lower values preserve structure; higher values create bigger changes.
ControlNet uses the selected conditioning model plus your input image to preserve structure more tightly. It is useful when you want pose, edges, or layout guidance.
LoRA applies a lightweight fine-tune on top of the base model. Use it to inject style or subject specialization without changing the main checkpoint.
Upscale needs a source image and an installed ESRGAN upscaler model. The app first fits the image to the working size, then applies the model factor the requested number of times.
Upscale factor is inferred from the model name, such as 2x or 4x. It tells you the base multiplier each pass applies.
Upscale repeats run the upscaler more than once. This can produce much larger outputs, but every extra pass increases time and the chance of over-sharpening or artificial detail.
The output resolution preview estimates the final size after fitting and repeated upscaling so you know what to expect before you launch the run.

Some image generation controls still live in Image Generation Settings instead of the main screen: default thread counts per mode, VAE tiling, VAE tile overlap, VAE tile size, relative tile size, and tensor type rules. Those settings still affect txt2img and img2img runs even though the quick-run screen stays focused on per-run choices.
Low-RAM guidance for devices under 8 GB

Start with classic SD 1.x or SDXL checkpoints, Z-Image, or quantized FLUX / Qwen variants before trying the largest full-precision diffusion stacks. Upstream memory notes make Z-Image and quantized FLUX variants especially practical for lower-memory devices.

Turn on mmap first. If a family supports it, try diffusion-fa next, then TAE or TAESD, then vae-conv-direct if decode memory is still too high. VAE tiling is the next lever when final decode is the unstable part.
Keep resolutions modest and do not overspend on steps. A lower resolution with a sensible step count is usually better on a phone than a huge canvas that forces swapping, overheating, or app kills.
txt2vid creates a video clip directly from text. Use it when you want the model to invent both the visual scene and the motion.
img2vid starts from a single input image and animates it toward your prompt. It is useful when you want to preserve the starting composition while adding motion.
The video model is the main diffusion model used for the run. Only models marked as video generation models are shown here.
VAE is optional in the UI, but many video model stacks expect an external VAE for decoding. Disable it only if your model setup truly does not need one.
T5-XXL is the large text encoder used by many current video models. If prompt understanding looks weak, missing T5 is often the first thing to check.
The prompt should describe the subject, environment, motion, mood, and camera feel. Clear motion language often matters more in video than it does in still images.
Negative prompt helps remove unwanted motion, color treatment, blur, artifacts, or low-quality traits from the generated clip.
Input image is only used for img2vid. The app prepares it the same way as image generation so you can reuse the same image-input workflow without reinventing it.
Frames and FPS together define the clip length and motion cadence. More frames can produce longer, richer motion but cost more time and memory.
Width and height define the render resolution. Larger resolutions can look better but are much heavier, especially on video models.
Steps control denoising length, while CFG scale controls prompt pressure. More is not always better; extreme values can slow generation or make motion unstable.
Sampling method changes the denoising algorithm. Different video models can prefer different samplers, so this is worth experimenting with instead of always staying on Euler.
Threads control how many CPU threads stable-diffusion.cpp may use. Auto or lower values can be more battery-friendly; higher values may help speed on some devices.
Flow shift is an optional video-model parameter exposed only when you enable it. Use it only when you know your chosen model benefits from tuning temporal flow behavior.
VAE tiling reduces decode memory pressure by processing the VAE in tiles. Tile size only matters when tiling is enabled.
diffusion-fa enables diffusion flash attention when supported, while mmap allows memory-mapped model loading. Both are performance-oriented runtime flags rather than creative controls.

Caching reuses diffusion work between steps to speed up generation. Current video models in the app are DiT-oriented, so easycache, dbcache, taylorseer, cache-dit, and spectrum are the usual starting points.
Cache option is passed to stable-diffusion.cpp exactly as typed, so you can use any supported key=value pair combination for the selected cache mode.
SCM policy and SCM mask only apply to cache-dit. They control which steps may cache and whether those steps always cache or still check thresholds dynamically.
Keeps same layer assignment for worker cache
This summary is used by the AI as long-term context.
Allow or block extended reasoning for Tama chat replies.
llama-server uses the same server model for the pet and the summarizer.
📜 Summary Updated: %1$s\'s memories have been condensed.
Unlocks the well so it can store water over time.
Unlocks the composter so rotten crops can turn into fertilizer.
Tilled a farm tile with hoe
Used 1 unit of water from the well
Revived a decayed plant with fertilizer!
A new egg appeared! It\'s %1$s the %2$s!
%s stayed up all night and is MAD! (Miscare +1) 😠
I\'m sleepy… put me to bed! 🥱
Finished Pomodoro study and gained %1$d education. Labels: %2$s.
Stopped study and gained %1$d education. Labels: %2$s.
You do not have any %1$s to eat.
Only teens and adults can work!
Only child, teen, and senior pets can study!
The app requires additional AI models and components to function properly. Please download them now.

Video upscaling needs the upscaler runtime module and its transient model pack. The models are extracted into app storage and the Play-managed pack is removed afterwards so future app updates stay lighter.
⚠️ This will download approximately 550 MB of data. Wi-Fi recommended.
The video upscaler needs its runtime before it can start.
Required AI models and support files
Native executables and libraries delivered as the feature module
Transient Play Asset Delivery pack extracted into app storage
Cleaning up the temporary Play-managed model pack
Core components are missing. You must download them to use the app.
Upscaler runtime models are still missing.
Setting up local AI components for privacy…
Use the selected Ollama server via /api/create. Use Ollama model names or explicit `hf.co/...` references here. Raw Hugging Face resolve URLs are not accepted.

Use the local Termux/SSH Ollama CLI. This mode preserves the full edited Modelfile and can use local GGUF paths, but raw Hugging Face resolve URLs must still be replaced with an explicit `hf.co/...` reference or a local file path.
Remote API mode cannot safely use these directives: %1$s. Switch to Local CLI to preserve the full Modelfile.
Remote API mode cannot use this FROM source: %1$s. %2$s
Local CLI mode cannot use this FROM source yet: %1$s. %2$s
Detected a raw Hugging Face GGUF URL. Replace it with the explicit Ollama-compatible reference `%1$s` before creating the model.
Local CLI mode requires an active Termux SSH connection on this device.
Use a model name without spaces or control characters. Allowed characters: letters, numbers, `.`, `_`, `-`, `/`, and an optional `:tag`.
Pull a model to get started
An egg has appeared! Give it a name:
The installed stable-diffusion.cpp binary does not support: %1$s
These components are filtered for the selected family: %1$s
Select the required components before generating: %1$s
No compatible image LLM models installed
Enable compatible ControlNet and LoRA add-ons for the selected family.
These advanced flags are shown only when the selected family supports them.
Different image families need different side models. This section surfaces only the compatible VAE, TAE, CLIP, T5, LLM, projector, and PhotoMaker components for the selected main model.
Reference-image editing families use the image as a reference instead of classic img2img strength blending. These models usually hide strength and rely on their own edit path.
Optional variant, for example sdxl, schnell, 2511
Comma-separated family tokens, for example flux_2,qwen_image_edit:2511
Pick one or more family profiles that this side model should be allowed to pair with. Tap a selected chip to remove it.
Your pet could use a little attention.
Your pet could use a cleanup.
Tap to open Tama and check on them.
Alerts when your Tama pet needs attention.
Alerts when Tama crops are ready.
Alerts when Tama makes a mess or leaves it too long.
Shows a live hh:mm sleep timer for Tama.
Alerts when Pomodoro study phases change or complete.
The pet is not currently studying.
The pet is studying in %1$s mode. Current phase: %2$s. Selected labels: %3$s.
The alchemist can brew stage, species, and growth potions.
Choose a potion to buy. Potions go into the main-room food/use menu, and a magic cloud will cover Tama when the change happens.
That potion could not be found.
That potion is not in the inventory.
That stage potion cannot be used.
That species potion cannot be used.
That healing potion cannot be used.
%1$s will now stay in this growth stage.
%1$s can evolve again from the start of this stage.
Met %1$s at the park. They said: “%2$s”
%1$s used %2$s and reset the stage timer for %3$s.
%1$s used %2$s and resumed automatic growth from the start of the stage.
Moves Tama to baby and restarts that stage timer.
Moves Tama to child and restarts that stage timer.
Moves Tama to teen and restarts that stage timer.
Moves Tama to adult and restarts that stage timer.
Moves Tama to senior and restarts that stage timer.
Changes the species to dragon and keeps the current stage.
Changes the species to kitsune and keeps the current stage.
Changes the species to unicorn and keeps the current stage.
Stops automatic growth at the current stage.
Restarts growth from the start of this stage.
Fully restores health up to the maximum.
Check the board for daily crop requests from park friends.
That quest is not available anymore.
You still need the requested crops.
Quest items added to the checklist.
That quest is already in the checklist.
Track the total items you need across quests. You can edit, check off, or delete each row.
No checklist items yet. Add a quest from the board or add an item manually.
No checked checklist items to clear.
That item cannot be tracked for quests.
That checklist item no longer exists.
%1$s accepted a quest from %2$s for %3$s and will earn %4$d coins.
Empty. Visit the shop to stock up.
The well slowly stores water over time.
Collect stored well water first, then use it from inventory with the watering can.
Rotten crops become fertilizer after enough time passes.
No Tama artwork yet. Dreams and school paintings will appear here.
This is what I saw while I was sleeping.
I dreamed of all this today...
Here are the moments that stayed with me after sleeping.
This is what I made while I was away studying.
Record a short message, then Whisper will transcribe it and send it to Tama.
Voice recorded. You can send it or try again.
Whisper could not understand that audio.
Record audio in Tama chat, choose the Whisper model and language, and let Whisper turn your voice into a message for Tama.
Enable image attachments only when the current Tama chat model can accept images as input.
Image input is disabled for the current Tama chat model.
Your audio was sent. Whisper is transcribing it in the background.
The test deep-dream button only works while Tama is sleeping.
Could not force deep dreaming right now.
No deep dream was queued for this sleep.
A deep-dream step timed out after %1$d minutes.
Deep dreaming took too long and was stopped after %1$d hours.
Deep-dream planning stalled and was cancelled.
The deep-dream album was never queued.
The deep-dream album slides are missing.
A deep-dream slide failed to render.
Deep-dream artwork stalled and was cancelled.
The saved deep-dream retry no longer matches the current sleep.
A local llama-server model is required for deep dreaming.
The local llama-server could not be started for deep dreaming.
The deep-dream backend did not connect after %1$d attempts.
%1$s could not start deep dreaming in background. Open P.E.T. to retry safely.
Generate one normal dream image when your pet goes to sleep, even during the day.\n\nThis uses the original Tama sleep-dream prompt and only needs a valid ONNX txt2img model.

Generate a deeper nightly dream recap with the pet memories.\n\nTama needs a configured Ollama or llama-server backend and a valid ONNX txt2img model. If Tama falls asleep between 21:00 and 03:00 and stays asleep for at least 4 hours, deep dreaming replaces the normal dream image from that sleep with a four-scene recap and refreshes the morning summary.
Choose the language for the deep-dream story and slide text. Tama still writes image prompts in English so the generator follows the expected format.
How many times Tama should retry each deep-dream writing step if the model answers badly.
Generate a school painting when your pet starts studying.
Used for dreams and school paintings. Tama always uses 20 steps, CFG 6.5, and a random seed.
Install DreamShaper · ManuXD32 if you want the default Tama image model.
The selected Tama image model is no longer installed.
The dream recap response was not valid.
No important events were recorded for that day.
No previous error details are available.

Write one daily dream recap for a virtual pet. The story and moment titles must be written in %1$s. Every image prompt must stay in English. Reply with JSON only, with no markdown fences and no explanation outside the JSON. Use this exact shape: {\"story\":\"...\",\"closing\":\"...\",\"moments\":[{\"title\":\"...\",\"prompt\":\"retro handheld game background illustration, ...\"},{\"title\":\"...\",\"prompt\":\"retro handheld game background illustration, ...\"},{\"title\":\"...\",\"prompt\":\"retro handheld game background illustration, ...\"},{\"title\":\"...\",\"prompt\":\"retro handheld game background illustration, ...\"}]}

Pet name: %1$s. Species style: %2$s. Life stage: %3$s. Personality: %4$s. Inventory now: %5$s. Day to recap: %6$s. Events from that day: %7$s. Desired language for the story and slide text: %8$s. This is attempt %9$s of %10$s. If a previous attempt failed, the parser error was: %11$s. Choose the four most meaningful moments from the day. Write a warm intro story for slide 1, a short closing recap for slide 6, and four slide texts for the moment slides. For each moment, create a different image prompt in English in the same style as this example: retro handheld game background illustration, charming 1990s Tamagotchi-style farm field scene, flat colorful 2d art, golden crop rows ready for harvest, wooden crate baskets placed near the center, small red barn on the left, windmill in the distance on the right, scarecrow near the back, dirt path leading through the field, soft rolling hills background, warm sunny daytime mood, cute toy-like shapes, bold outlines, simple readable composition, clear open center space for pet tiles, no text, no UI, no people, no foreground objects.
Tama remembered the day, but this slide had no extra text.
Tap an empty tile to add a crop, or tap a ready tile to collect fertilizer.
Fertilizer is ready to collect from this slot.
This crop is still turning into fertilizer.
This slot is available for one crop.
No compostable crops are available right now.
The well now makes water every %1$d hours.
That job is not available right now.
You need %1$d study points to work as %2$s.
Study points: %1$d. Better jobs unlock as your pet learns more.
That toy could not be found.
That toy is not in the inventory.
Placed %1$s on the %2$s side of the room.
Removed %1$s from the %2$s side of the room.
%1$s is back in the inventory from the %2$s side.
That room slot is already empty.
Width and height must be divisible by 8.
., app, scripts, or another path inside the current project
Custom tools are confined to the current project. Prefix the command template with shell: only when you intentionally need high-risk shell mode.
Provide a JSON array of tool names. Leave it empty to use the safe default tool set for custom agents.
Allow this agent to inspect images when the backend model supports vision.
Shared ONNX image-generation settings used by the built-in generate_image tool.
Allow agents to create ONNX images and save them into the workspace.
Separate ONNX settings used only by the native chat generate_image tool.
Select an installed ONNX text-to-image model in Image Generation Settings before using native chat image generation.
Could not attach the selected image.
The agent wants to create this folder:\n%1$s
The agent wants to generate an image and save it to:\n%1$s
Select an installed ONNX text-to-image model in Agent Settings before using generate_image.
The generate_image tool is disabled in Agent Settings.
The configured image-generation resolution is invalid.
llama-server uses the server-owned active model and context. Refresh the metadata above if it changes.
Optionally generate one local ONNX image for the opening world before the first scene is written.
Optionally generate a local ONNX image for every new stage after each player choice.
Your new adventure world is ready to play.
The next adventure scene is ready.
The world outline could not be generated.
The opening scene could not be generated.
The next scene could not be generated.
No active adventure was found for this dungeon.
Create a pet before starting an adventure.
Waiting for the world to finish building…
This run has reached its ending. You can reset the world to start a new story.
Adds 1 cow to the barn. Current occupancy: %1$d / %2$d.
Adds 1 chicken to the coop. Current occupancy: %1$d / %2$d.
Feed mode on: tap hungry animals to feed them wheat.
You need wheat to feed this animal.
This animal is not hungry right now.
Bought a cow for the barn.
Bought a chicken for the coop.
Collected %1$d milk bottle(s) from the barn!
Collect the stored %1$s before more gets tossed.
%1$d hungry animal(s) are waiting for wheat.
%1$s smiles: \"Thanks so much. Here are your %2$d coins.\"
%1$s nods happily: \"Just what I needed. Take %2$d coins.\"
%1$s beams: \"You saved the day. Please accept %2$d coins.\"
%1$s says: \"Everything arrived safely. %2$d coins for you.\"
%1$s grins: \"Amazing work. I owe you %2$d coins.\"
%1$s says: \"That was fast. Enjoy these %2$d coins.\"
%1$s thanks you warmly: \"Please take %2$d coins for your help.\"
%1$s says: \"Exactly what I asked for. Here are %2$d coins.\"
Home screen shortcuts are not available on this launcher.
Shows an interactive Organizer month with events on each day.
Shows the next Organizer events in colored rows.
Shows one selected Organizer note in a scrollable widget with interactive checklist items.
This note is no longer available.
Pick the note this widget should keep visible on your home screen.
Shows and replies to a native Llama chat.
Pick the native Llama chat this widget should open and reply to.
